entities.name: PIGEONPEA
entities.description: Cajanus cajan (L.) Millsp., the crop studied for hybrid development

entities.name: CYTOPLASMIC-NUCLEAR MALE STERILITY
entities.description: A system of male sterility used to produce hybrid seeds

entities.name: PARTIAL NATURAL OUTCROSSING
entities.description: A breeding technique used in conjunction with CMS for hybrid production

entities.name: ICRISAT
entities.description: The International Crops Research Institute for the Semi-Arid Tropics, where the breeding technology was developed

entities.name: HYBRID VIGOR
entities.description: The tendency of a hybrid offspring to exhibit qualities superior to those of its parents

entities.name: SEED YIELD
entities.description: The amount of seed produced per unit area

entities.name: FERTILITY RESTORATION
entities.description: The genetic process of restoring male fertility in CMS hybrids

entities.name: A4 CMS SYSTEM
entities.description: A specific cytoplasmic-nuclear male sterility system used in pigeonpea

entities.name: POLLEN FERTILITY
entities.description: The ability of pollen grains to successfully fertilize the ovule

entities.name: MALE FERTILITY
entities.description: The capacity of the male reproductive organ to produce viable pollen

entities.name: SINGLE DOMINANT GENE
entities.description: A single gene that controls fertility restoration in extra-early maturing hybrids

entities.name: TWO DUPLICATE DOMINANT GENES
entities.description: Two genes that govern male fertility in early and late maturing hybrids

entities.name: POLLEN LOAD
entities.description: The quantity of pollen produced by the plant

entities.name: STABILITY
entities.description: The consistency of fertility restoration across different conditions
